video_title: Guy Fieri Digs Into Massive BBQ Plates in Portland 🍖 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Matt's Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in northeast Portland, Oregon, in the parking lot of a pawn shop, and this story just gets weirder. You see, there's a dude from Long Island, and he moves all the way to Australia to study Texas barbecue. That's right, then he comes back with his Australian wife. Well, they travel across country to the west coast, and they open up this joint. This is Matt's Barbecue. All the hype is for Matt Vicciadomini, who ditched New York City fine dining to head down under. Portland is not the south, but Matt's bringing it right here in a food cart.

dishes.description: Pork spare ribs trimmed, seasoned with Texas-style salt and pepper rub, smoked for 4-6 hours at 275F. Glazed with a smoked garlic jelly (onions, garlic, vinegar, sugar) and barbecue sauce (ketchup, jelly, Worcestershire). Rested for at least 30 minutes. Served with potato salad and pinto beans.
dishes.guy_response: Good rib, buddy. Lightly sauced. Lightly glazed. Pulled apart tender. Potato salad's spot on. The beans are mealing themselves. So, so, so good. Some of the best I've eaten. And I've eaten ribs in 37 states.

dishes.description: A massive sandwich featuring all their smoked meats: pulled pork (seasoned with chipotle vinegar, salt, sugar), sausage (rib and brisket trimmings, salt, pepper, paprika, cayenne, fresh garlic), and brisket (salt, pepper, mustard). Topped with pickles, pickled red onion, barbecue sauce, coleslaw, and a skewer with a rib.
dishes.guy_response: That's just flat out ridiculous. I don't know exactly how to attack this. This definitely deserves a hunch. It's dynamite, man. Well, I'm going to tell you right now, the sausage is outrageous. Look at just the juice that comes out of that sausage. The brisket's spot on. A little vinegar chipotle kick from the pulled pork. You're not hiding any of the barbecue in a bunch of sauce. Shabamwich. The pulled pork is fantastic. The sausage is the best. The brisket's world class. Some of the best I've ever eaten.

restaurants.name: Slow Bone
restaurants.guy_intro: Here in Dallas, Texas, love this city, great food. But when I roll into town, I want barbecue. Now, there's Texas barbecue. Then there's joints that do Texas, Kansas City, Carolina barbecue. But I hear about this place where the chef is kind of putting the world of food together inside a barbecue and chefing it up. This is Slow Bone.

dishes.description: A gourmet brisket sandwich. Brisket is trimmed, seasoned with a rub of roasted coffee, black pepper, granulated garlic, chipotle powder, and granulated onion, then smoked with post-oak and hickory. It's rested in rendered smoked fat trimmings. Served with smoked mushrooms, caramelized onions, beer cheese, green chili, red onion, lime crema, and jalapeno barbecue sauce on toasted buns with Duke's mayo.
dishes.guy_response: This is hillbilly beef wellington. You've got the crust. You have the duxelle. The brisket is so unbelievably tender. The creaminess of the cheese, it's so succulent, juicy, unctuous. It's a gourmet meal disguised as a brisket barbecue sandwich. Nailed it. It's got the sweetness. The heat to it. It's just so juicy. It kind of just melts in your mouth. Creamo.

restaurants.name: The Pig Witch
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Kansas City, Missouri. You do not roll into this town without calling the head coach of the Kansas City Chiefs, Coach Reed, and saying, listen, buddy, where are we going to eat? We're going to a butcher shop that's also the greatest sandwich shop you've ever eaten at. The real deal. What's it called? The Pig Witch. Over at the local pig section, you can get all of your locally sourced meats. Pig Witch is the restaurant portion. You can get sandwiches, burgers. It's all good Kansas City growth, man.

dishes.description: A large sandwich featuring a patty made from smoked pork butt, ground pork, bacon trimmings, and house barbecue rub. Also includes breaded and fried pork tenderloin (brined in buttermilk). Served with three cheeses (pepper jack, cheddar, provolone), house ranch dressing, andouille spread, house barbecue sauce, and B&B jalapenos.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. I love the crisp on the pork, and I'm loving the patty. Three cheeses, man, blend perfect. How about the jalapenos? Man, a lot. Bread and butter jalapenos is my new favorite thing. The ranch dressing, you need that cooling, creaming effect. My favorite. Part of the whole thing, though, is the pulled pork patty. My favorite part is eating it.

restaurants.name: ZZQ
restaurants.guy_intro: You see, I have a responsibility to bring you some of the best barbecue you've ever seen on Triple D. And after this spot, you'll say, I thank you. This is ZZQ.

dishes.description: A large sandwich featuring Fontina habanero sausage, pulled pork (smoked with a dry rub and mustard/pickle brine slather), and beef brisket (smoked with a dry rub and mustard/pickle brine slather). Topped with a signature house thick sauce (made with toasted chilies, caramelized onions, molasses, and various spices) and pickled red onions.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious brisket. Love the sausage. Pulled pork's nice and tender. It's legit. It's real deal. It's great flavor. Well done, chef. You have to really press it down to fit in your mouth. The brisket is very tender. It's really delicious.

restaurants.name: Meat Boss
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm on the west side of Mobile, Alabama, to check out a barbecue joint. And I know what you're thinking. Barbecue joint? Aren't there hundreds of them? Well, on Triple D, it's all about the food, the flavor. And the character and the story. And the character and story in this are big. 10 years ago, this guy fires up this barbecue joint. Never been in the restaurant business before. Just does things his way. Unfortunately, he passes away. Well, wife takes over the front of the house. Daughter becomes the head cook. Well, if you're hungry, this is Meat Boss.

dishes.description: A burger made from hand-ground brisket and chuck, seasoned with salt, granulated garlic, black pepper, granulated onion, Worcestershire, liquid smoke, and burger seasoning. Grilled and served on a toasted bun with spicy Carolina sauce (a sweet sauce with cayenne and vinegar) and rib candy (rib meat mixed with honey, brown sugar, and sweet sauce). Topped with cheddar cheese.
dishes.guy_response: The burger is really good. The sauce is great. I'm getting good smoke off the ribs. I thought it was too much sauce, because it's only sauce, burger, and rib. A little bit of cheese. But there's sauce. There's so much flavor in it. And you never see rib meat by itself. I get after this thing. I love the rib candy. It is so juicy, tender, just smoky enough, and not too smoky. With a little bit of sweet twist. Scrumptious.
